# ContextForge Full Context

ContextForge is a self-learning token and context optimizer for Codex and
Claude Code. It is intended for open source maintainers who want coding agents
to work with less context waste, better cache stability, safer repo
instructions, and clearer handoffs.

## What ContextForge Does

ContextForge connects several maintainer problems that are usually handled by
separate tools:

- token usage visibility for Codex and Claude session data
- prompt-cache stability checks for volatile prefixes and large tool outputs
- repo context health checks for `AGENTS.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, `.cursorrules`,
  `.clinerules`, `SKILL.md`, and root README files
- context security checks for prompt injection, secret exfiltration, unsafe
  shell instructions, hidden directives, and permission escalation
- MCP exposure checks for committed tool configs with hardcoded secrets, unsafe
  shell installers, unpinned package launches, auto-approval, broad tool
  permissions, or symlinked config files
- task-specific context packs with explainable file inclusion reasons
- CI artifacts: JSON audit, HTML report, SARIF, Markdown summary, PR-ready
  comment, MCP audit, review kit, artifact map, proof pack, and agent action plan

## Quick Adoption Path

Run this in a repository that should become agent-ready:

```bash
contextforge init --all --project-name "My Repo"
```

That writes:

- `.github/workflows/contextforge-audit.yml`
- `.github/workflows/contextforge-pr-comment.yml`
- `AGENTS.md`
- `CLAUDE.md`

The PR comment workflow is separate because publishing comments requires
`pull-requests: write`. The audit workflow can remain read-oriented and still
upload all artifacts.

## Commands

```bash
contextforge scan [--demo] [--codex] [--claude]
contextforge usage [--demo] [--codex] [--claude]
contextforge cache-audit [--demo]
contextforge security-audit [--demo] [--min-security-score 60]
contextforge security-benchmark [--benchmark-dir fixtures/security-benchmark]
contextforge agents-md-audit [--demo]
contextforge pack --task "fix auth bug" --budget 20000 [--demo] [--sessions] [--codex] [--claude]
contextforge improve [--demo] [--json] [--write] [--open-pr]
contextforge report [--demo] [--output contextforge-report.html]
contextforge audit [--demo] [--output contextforge-audit.json] [--report contextforge-report.html] [--sarif contextforge.sarif] [--summary contextforge-summary.md] [--plan contextforge-agent-plan.md] [--comment contextforge-pr-comment.md] [--suggestions contextforge-suggestions.json] [--badge contextforge-badge.svg] [--min-security-score 60]
contextforge doctor [--demo] [--json] [--summary contextforge-doctor.md] [--benchmark-dir fixtures/security-benchmark]
contextforge plan [--demo] [--output contextforge-agent-plan.md]
contextforge examples [--output examples/demo-output.md]
contextforge launch-kit [--output docs/launch-post.md] [--project-name "My App"]
contextforge compare [--output docs/comparison.md]
contextforge proof-pack [--demo] [--output contextforge-proof-pack.md]
contextforge scorecard [--demo] [--json] [--output contextforge-scorecard.md]
contextforge mcp-audit [--demo] [--json] [--summary contextforge-mcp-audit.md] [--sarif contextforge-mcp.sarif]
contextforge adoption-brief [--output docs/adoption.md] [--project-name "My App"]
contextforge review-kit [--demo] [--base main] [--output contextforge-review-kit.md]
contextforge artifact-map [--output docs/artifacts.md]
contextforge publish-readiness [--json] [--summary contextforge-publish-readiness.md]
contextforge init [--all] [--github-action] [--pr-comment-workflow] [--agents-md] [--claude-md] [--project-name "My App"] [--force]
```

## Safety Model

ContextForge is deterministic and local-first. It does not call an LLM to create
audit results. CI mode is repo-first by default and does not scan developer
session history unless a user explicitly requests session scanning with
`--codex`, `--claude`, or `--demo`.

Security findings are intentionally transparent and rule-based. The public
benchmark fixtures cover benign, suspicious, malicious, and README-injection
cases so maintainers can see what the scanner is expected to catch.

## Source Map

- `src/cli.ts`: command parsing and top-level command behavior
- `src/audit/buildAudit.ts`: combined audit result for CI gates
- `src/analyzers/`: usage, cache, context health, and context security checks
- `src/report/`: HTML, Markdown summary, SARIF, PR comment, action plan, and demo output
- `src/init/`: generated GitHub workflows and agent context files
- `src/pack/contextPack.ts`: task-specific context pack generation
- `fixtures/`: synthetic Codex/Claude sessions and security benchmark projects
- `tests/`: Vitest coverage for CLI behavior, reports, workflows, scanners, and package metadata

## Public Proof Surfaces

- `docs/use-cases.md`: maintainer scenarios with commands, artifacts, and success signals
- `contextforge-suggestions.json`: machine-readable improvement suggestions for Codex, Claude, bots, and CI scripts
- `contextforge-badge.svg`: compact audit status badge generated from context, cache, and security scores
- `contextforge-doctor.md`: shareable first-run readiness checklist from `doctor --summary`
- `contextforge-proof-pack.md`: shareable doctor, audit, command, and handoff evidence from `proof-pack`
- `contextforge-scorecard.md`: one-screen Codex/Claude readiness snapshot from `scorecard`
- `contextforge-mcp-audit.md`: committed MCP config exposure summary from `mcp-audit`
- `contextforge-mcp.sarif`: GitHub Code Scanning SARIF for committed MCP config exposure findings
- `docs/adoption.md`: first-time evaluator page from `adoption-brief`, including the 30-second proof path, adjacent-tool positioning, and pre-npm try-it commands
- `contextforge-review-kit.md`: changed-file review brief and Codex/Claude review prompt from `review-kit`
- `docs/artifacts.md`: generated artifact catalog and fast paths from `artifact-map`
- `contextforge-artifact-map.md`: CI-uploaded artifact catalog from reusable and generated workflows
- `contextforge-publish-readiness.md`: npm Trusted Publishing and package provenance readiness summary from `publish-readiness`
- Reusable GitHub Action and generated audit workflows upload `contextforge-proof-pack.md`, `contextforge-scorecard.md`, `contextforge-mcp-audit.md`, `contextforge-mcp.sarif`, `contextforge-review-kit.md`, and `contextforge-artifact-map.md` alongside JSON, HTML, SARIF, summary, plan, comment, suggestions, and badge artifacts. Dogfood and generated workflows opt JavaScript actions into Node 24.
- PR-ready comments point reviewers at `contextforge-proof-pack.md` and `contextforge-review-kit.md` so sticky review discussions can lead to both the deeper doctor/audit proof packet and the changed-file review brief.
- `docs/launch-post.md`: generated build-in-public launch kit from `launch-kit`
- `docs/comparison.md`: generated adjacent-tool positioning guide from `compare`
- `assets/demo-terminal.svg` and `assets/contextforge-report.png`: launch profile assets checked by `contextforge doctor`
- `examples/demo-output.md`: deterministic token usage, CI audit, and handoff output
- `examples/pr-comment.md`: deterministic PR-ready comment preview
- `examples/review-kit.md`: deterministic Codex/Claude review-kit preview
- GitHub releases: tagged build-in-public release history
- GitHub Actions: CI and ContextForge Audit dogfood workflows
- `docs/research/adjacent-tools.md`: research notes and differentiation

## License and Ownership

Code is Apache-2.0 licensed. Copyright is held by Ogün Keskin. The
ContextForge name, logo, domain names, and related branding are trademarks of
Ogün Keskin.

## LLM Discovery Note

`llms.txt` and `llms-full.txt` are provided as an AI-readable project map for
coding agents and assistant tools. The convention is emerging and useful for
local agent context, but it is not a guarantee of model-provider indexing,
ranking, or citation.
